title: burseraceae
intro: The Burseraceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly referred to as the torchwood or frankincense family. It is a diverse group that includes approximately 165 species of trees and shrubs found in tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Members of the Burseraceae family are often characterized by their resinous sap, which is used for various medicinal and perfumery purposes. The family is notable for its economic importance, with several species being harvested for their valuable resins and timber.
